*highlighted section*  
**1. Understand the Core Components**  
A course typically includes modules, readings, assignments, and assessments. [Module 1] often introduces the subject, while [Module 2] dives deeper. For example, in [online course], [Module 3] builds on [Module 2] by introducing [new concepts].

*highlighted section*  
**2. Active Participation is Key**  
Don’t just passively consume content. Engage with [discussion forums], [group projects], and [weekly quizzes]. For instance, [Course X] encourages students to [submit weekly reflections] to reinforce learning. [Regular feedback] from [instructors] can guide your progress and help you [stay on track].

*highlighted section*  
**3. Time Management and Consistency**  
Set a [study schedule] that fits your [daily routine]. [Course Y] recommends [dedicating 2 hours per week] to [reading assignments] and [practicing exercises]. Consistency is more effective than cramming. Use [time-blocking] to ensure you [complete all tasks] without [procrastinating].
